title: Dates and times
summary: Learn how to store and work with dates and times in SQL tables. Understand the common data types, formatting, and best practices for handling date and time values in your database schema.

sketchBlocks.title: Storing Dates and Times in SQL
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
Dates and times are essential for tracking when records are created, updated, or relevant. In SQLite, dates and times are often stored as TEXT in the format 'YYYY-MM-DD' or 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'. For example, the `products` table in the `products_catalog` database has a `created_at` column:

```sql
CREATE TABLE products (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  category TEXT NOT NULL,
  price REAL NOT NULL,
  stock INTEGER NOT NULL,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL
);
```

Sample row:

| id | name             | category   | price | stock | created_at  |
|----|------------------|------------|-------|-------|-------------|
| 1  | Sketchbook       | Art        | 12.99 | 25    | 2026-01-10  |

This format makes it easy to sort and filter by date.

sketchBlocks.title: Why Use TEXT for Dates?
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
SQLite does not have a dedicated DATE or DATETIME type. Instead, dates and times are usually stored as TEXT, REAL, or INTEGER. The most common approach is to use TEXT in the ISO 8601 format ('YYYY-MM-DD' or 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'), which sorts correctly and is easy to read.

For example, to find all products added after January 31, 2026:

```sql
SELECT * FROM products WHERE created_at > '2026-01-31';
```

This query works because the TEXT format allows for correct chronological comparison.

quizDraft.title: Select products created after a specific date
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL query to select the name and created_at columns for all products that were created after January 31, 2026.
quizDraft.hint: Use a WHERE clause to compare the created_at column to the date string.

help.concept: To filter rows by date, use a WHERE clause comparing the date column to a string in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format.
help.hint_1: Remember to use the correct column name and date format.
help.hint_2: The WHERE clause should check if created_at is greater than '2026-01-31'.

quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT name, created_at FROM products WHERE created_at > '2026-01-31';

quizDraft.title: Add a new product with today's date
quizDraft.prompt: Insert a new product called 'Watercolor Set' in the 'Art' category, priced at 19.99, with 20 in stock, and a created_at date of '2026-03-01'.
quizDraft.hint: Use an INSERT INTO statement with all columns specified.

help.concept: INSERT INTO adds a new row to the table. Make sure to provide values for all NOT NULL columns.
help.hint_1: List all columns in the INSERT statement, including created_at.

quizDraft.solutionCode: INSERT INTO products (name, category, price, stock, created_at) VALUES ('Watercolor Set', 'Art', 19.99, 20, '2026-03-01');
quizDraft.checkSql: SELECT name, category, price, stock, created_at FROM products WHERE name = 'Watercolor Set' AND created_at = '2026-03-01';

quizDraft.title: Update the created_at date for a product
quizDraft.prompt: The 'Desk Lamp' product was entered with the wrong created_at date. Update its created_at value to '2026-02-20'.
quizDraft.hint: Use an UPDATE statement to change the created_at value for the correct product.

help.concept: UPDATE changes values in existing rows. Use WHERE to target the correct row.
help.hint_1: Match the product by its name in the WHERE clause.
help.hint_2: Set created_at to '2026-02-20' for the row where name is 'Desk Lamp'.

quizDraft.solutionCode: UPDATE products SET created_at = '2026-02-20' WHERE name = 'Desk Lamp';
quizDraft.checkSql: SELECT name, created_at FROM products WHERE name = 'Desk Lamp' AND created_at = '2026-02-20';

quizDraft.title: Common ways to store dates in SQLite
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following are common ways to store date and time values in SQLite? Select all that apply.
quizDraft.hint: Think about the data types SQLite supports for storing dates.

help.concept: SQLite allows dates to be stored as TEXT, REAL, or INTEGER, depending on the use case.
help.hint_1: Consider if storing as a formatted string, a number, or a timestamp is possible.
help.hint_2: More than one data type can be used for dates in SQLite.

quizDraft.title: Date format for sorting
quizDraft.prompt: Fill in the blank: The recommended format for storing dates as TEXT in SQLite is ____.
quizDraft.hint: It's a standard format that sorts correctly and is easy to compare.

help.concept: The ISO 8601 format 'YYYY-MM-DD' is recommended for storing dates as TEXT in SQLite.
help.hint_1: The format starts with the year, then month, then day.

quizDraft.template: The recommended format for storing dates as TEXT in SQLite is ____.
